concerns.text: FR-002 mandates using a method 'appropriate for the data distribution' but does not define the objective criteria or statistical test (e.g., Shapiro-Wilk p-value < 0.05) to trigger the switch from Pearson to Spearman. This makes the requirement non-deterministic and unverifiable.

FR-007 sets a 6-hour time limit on a 'standard CPU-only GitHub Actions runner' without specifying the exact runner type (e.g., ubuntu-latest vs. ubuntu-22.04) or CPU core count. Performance benchmarks vary significantly between runner types, making this threshold unmeasurable without a fixed environment definition.

The edge case regarding zero-inflated data states the system 'must automatically switch' to a zero-inflated model but fails to define the trigger condition (e.g., proportion of zeros > X% or a specific goodness-of-fit test result). Without a defined trigger, the behavior is not verifiable.

The edge case for sample size states the system must flag a 'Power Limitation' if N < 20. However, statistical power depends on effect size and variance, not just N. A fixed N threshold is scientifically arbitrary and may not accurately reflect power limitations, making the 'flag' condition unverifiable against the actual statistical power.

The outlier detection edge case specifies using the 'IQR method' but does not define the multiplier (e.g., 1.5x IQR vs. 3x IQR) or the action (exclude vs. cap). Different multipliers yield different results, making the requirement ambiguous and untestable.
